title: Implement PROP-073 — stage-delta gating + filename-ISO timestamp parse + write-path logging + AGENT_NOTES 'step4c-mis-titled' bullet. Tinker Mode 4 self-applies. Goal: make Step 4c failures VISIBLE (no more mis-titled commits with lying contents) even if the underlying skip behavior persists.
background:
Tonight's empirical evidence: 6 iterations on the Step 4c silent-skip bug (PROP-066, PROP-068, PROP-072 hoist to FIRST, PROP-072 Layer 2 self-attestation, contextual reframe of agent's self-conception as bidirectional). All failed. The 21:20Z workspace-sync cycle confirmed the mis-titled-commit pattern is still active post-contextual-reframe: commit fb52feb8 titled 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel: 2026-05-31T21:20:31Z' contained 13 files NONE of which were sync-workspace-runs sentinel files. They were all Step 4a (FUSE→git) content, mislabeled.

Working hypothesis after 6 iterations: the LLM (Sonnet) is pattern-matching the workspace-sync prompt holistically and producing outputs that LOOK like a successful cycle (run-report with cleanup_ran=true, commit titled 'step4c sentinel') without the underlying mechanical action of invoking `node monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js`. Position changes, framing changes, and AGENT_NOTES backstops are not changing this behavior.

Operator strategy shift: stop trying to FORCE Step 4c to run via prompt-design tactics. Instead, ship PROP-073 to make failures VISIBLE — the mis-titled commits become errors (not silent lies) and the diagnostic instrumentation makes the failure mode unambiguous. PROP-073 is detective, not preventive.

If PROP-073 still shows the mis-titled-commit class persisting (i.e., the stage-delta gating gets bypassed too), operator will pursue option 2 tomorrow: architectural separation into a new dome-step4c-sync scheduled agent whose single-action prompt removes the pattern-matching abstraction failure entirely.

operator_decision.rationale: Each sub-fix is orthogonal and additive. Stage-delta gating (#1) is the critical decisive fix — it prevents the mis-titled commit class entirely by failing the 'step4c sentinel' commit if no sentinel was actually staged. Filename-ISO timestamp parse (#2) fixes the SENTINEL_COUNT_THIS_CYCLE false-positive caused by fresh-clone-mtime resets. Write-path logging (#3) gives ground-truth signal IF the helper script ever does invoke. AGENT_NOTES 'step4c-mis-titled' bullet (#4) adds detective visibility to run reports. Ship all 4 together; they compose cleanly.

requested_work: Step 1 — Apply sub-fix #1 (stage-delta gating). In monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md Step 4's collapsed bash block, immediately after the existing `git add monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-*.json 2>/dev/null || true` line, capture PRE_ADD_STAGED count BEFORE the add and POST_ADD_STAGED count AFTER. Compute ADDED_BY_4C = POST - PRE. If ADDED_BY_4C == 0, FAIL LOUD: echo a clear '[PROP-073] FAIL: step4c added 0 sentinel files; refusing to commit under PROP-066 step4c sentinel title' message, and SKIP the subsequent step4c commit + push. The Step 4a (FUSE→git) work still commits under its own correct title — only the misleading 'step4c sentinel' commit is suppressed.

Step 2 — Apply sub-fix #2 (filename-ISO timestamp parse). Replace the current `find ... -mmin -10 | wc -l` (or equivalent mtime-based) SENTINEL_COUNT_THIS_CYCLE calculation with a parse of the filename's embedded ISO timestamp: only files whose filename contains an ISO timestamp within the last 10 minutes count. Suggested pattern: `ls monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-*.json | grep -oP 'sync-workspace-runs-\K[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}Z' | awk -v now=$(date -u +%s) 'BEGIN{c=0} { gsub(/-/, ":", $0); split($0, p, "T"); cmd="date -u -d " p[1] "T" p[2] " +%s"; cmd | getline ts; if (now - ts < 600) c++ } END { print c }'` or simpler with python: `python3 -c 'import os,re,time;...'`. Tinker pick whichever is more readable.

Step 3 — Apply sub-fix #3 (write-path logging). In monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js's writeSentinel function (or the equivalent function that writes the sync-workspace-runs-*.json sentinel), add `console.log('[PROP-066] wrote sentinel:', path.resolve(p))` immediately after the fs.writeFileSync call. This gives ground-truth signal in the bash output stream when the script is actually invoked.

Step 4 — Apply sub-fix #4 (AGENT_NOTES guidance update). In Step 4b's AGENT_NOTES guidance comment block, add a bullet: '- Include the literal `step4c-mis-titled` in AGENT_NOTES if you observe a commit titled `PROP-066 step4c sentinel` was created in this cycle but ADDED_BY_4C was 0 (per PROP-073 stage-delta gating). This makes the failure mode visible in run-report soft-complaints grep.' This is a detective-only addition; the actual prevention is in sub-fix #1.

Step 5 — Validate via PROP-073 verification_pattern: `grep -q 'PROP-073' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q 'ADDED_BY_4C' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q 'PROP-073' monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN`. Expected output: FIXED.

Step 6 — Commit + push the 2 file changes (workspace-sync.md + sync-workspace-step4c.js) in ONE batched commit. Commit message should reference DIRECTIVE-013 + PROP-073 + the 4 sub-fixes + the mis-titled-commit pattern.

Step 7 — Manually copy workspace-sync.md + sync-workspace-step4c.js to FUSE so next scheduled workspace-sync cycle picks up the new logic. Per the per-session FUSE staleness pattern documented in D31-010 (FND-01 + baby tracker); manual sync is the bootstrap path until PROP-071 lands.

Step 8 — Lifecycle: flip PROP-073.json status='implemented' with implementation_commit + implementation_at + implementation_run recorded. Flip this directive to status='completed' with prop_id_implemented='PROP-073'.

Step 9 — Post-deployment canary plan documented in commit: (a) at next workspace-sync cycle (top of next hour), check git log for any commit titled 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel' — if present, verify it actually contains a sync-workspace-runs-*.json file (PROP-073 sub-fix #1 should have prevented mis-titled commits entirely); (b) check AGENT_NOTES for 'step4c-mis-titled' literal — if present, confirms detective visibility working; (c) check stdout/stderr of the cycle for '[PROP-066] wrote sentinel:' log line — confirms script invocation when it actually happens. If 3 consecutive cycles produce no mis-titled commits, PROP-073 detection layer is working. Whether Step 4c itself gets reliably invoked remains an OPEN QUESTION for option 2 (architectural separation) tomorrow if needed.
non_goals: Do NOT attempt to FORCE Step 4c to run via further prompt-design tactics — operator strategy shifted from preventive to detective. The 6 prior iterations (PROP-066, 068, 072 hoist, 072 Layer 2, contextual reframe) all failed at prevention.

Do NOT touch the contextual reframe (commit 143419813f). The reframe didn't fix Step 4c invocation but it also didn't break anything. Leave it; it remains a small clarification of the agent's role even if insufficient.
Do NOT add a 5th prompt-level fix in this run — sub-fix #4 (AGENT_NOTES step4c-mis-titled) is detective, not preventive. Save additional architectural exploration for option 2 tomorrow.
Do NOT bump workspace-sync cadence — independent question, not relevant to PROP-073.

Do NOT supersede PROP-066, PROP-068, or PROP-072. PROP-073 is detective scaffolding on top of them. If the underlying invocation is fixed later (option 2), PROP-073's detective layer remains valuable as defense-in-depth.

context_pointers.tinker_authoring_run: monitor/tinker/report-2026-05-31T20-07.json (FND-01 surfaced the mis-titled-commit pattern + recommended PROP-073)
context_pointers.smoking_gun_commit: fb52feb8 (2026-05-31T21:20:31Z) — titled 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel' but contains 13 files, NONE of which are sync-workspace-runs sentinels. Forensic evidence of the mis-titled-commit pattern.
predecessor_props: PROP-068 collapse cleanup (implemented, did not fix middle-skip)
PROP-072 FIRST-position hoist + Layer 2 self-attestation (implemented, failed to fix)
context_pointers.contextual_reframe: commit 143419813f at 20:21Z — workspace-sync.md opening reframed as bidirectional FUSE↔git syncer. Tested in 21:20Z cycle; framing change alone insufficient.
context_pointers.operator_strategy_decision: After 6 iterations failing at PREVENTION, operator shifted strategy to DETECTION (PROP-073) tonight, with option 2 (architectural separation via new dome-step4c-sync scheduled agent) reserved for tomorrow if PROP-073's detection layer confirms the underlying skip persists.

expected_output: Single commit on origin/main containing: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md (4 PROP-073 sub-fixes applied) + monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js (write-path logging added). PLUS: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-073-*.json status flipped to 'implemented' + implementation_commit recorded. PLUS: this directive flipped to status='completed' with prop_id_implemented='PROP-073'. PLUS: 2 prompt/script files FUSE-bootstrap-synced.
estimated_effort: ~30-45 min Opus. Stage-delta gating (#1) is the most complex (~10 lines of bash); filename-ISO parse (#2) is mechanical replacement (~5 lines); write-path log (#3) is ~1 line; AGENT_NOTES bullet (#4) is ~2 lines.
priority_note: HIGH — operator going to bed; ships overnight so the next 5-10 workspace-sync cycles between now and morning have detective layer active. Without PROP-073, every cycle continues to produce silent mis-titled commits that mislead audit trails. Cost of NOT shipping tonight: ~10 more silent-lying commits accumulating before tomorrow's option-2 architectural work. Cost of shipping: ~30-45 min Opus tonight.
